title: Preparation of long rDNA amplicons of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi (Glomeromycota) from soil and root DNA for high-fidelity long-read sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9m4el","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of long rDNA amplicons (~2.7 kb) of arbuscular mycorrhizal (AM) fungi (phylum Glomeromycota) from environmental DNA for high-fidelity long-read sequencing. The workflow targets AMF communities in both soil and fine roots, using DNA extracted with common commercial kits. The method employs a nested PCR strategy adapted from Kolaříková et al. (2021, New Phytologist), which amplifies a contiguous fragment spanning most of the SSU rRNA gene, the complete ITS region, and the D1–D2 domains of the LSU rRNA gene. In the first round, broad AMF primer combinations NS31/LSUmAr13+24 and AML1/LSUmAr13+24 are used, following the LSUmAr13 + LSUmAr24 LSU primer design of Tedersoo et al. (2018, New Phytologist) rather than the original LSUmAr1–4 mix. This primer scheme provided robust amplification from diverse soil and root DNA extracts in test runs. The pooled first-round products are then subjected to a Glomeromycota-specific second PCR with NS31_Glo3/wLSUmBr, yielding ~2.7 kb amplicons that cover SSU–ITS–LSU for AMF lineages. The resulting libraries are suitable for downstream long-read sequencing on PacBio Revio/Sequel IIe or Oxford Nanopore platforms. Because the primer scheme is AMF-focused but not site-specific, the protocol can be applied to a wide range of soil- or root-associated AMF communities and integrated with existing SSU, ITS, and LSU datasets for phylogenetic and ecological analyses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4l5k4","text":"This protocol describes a nested PCR workflow for amplifying near-full-length SSU–ITS–LSU rDNA of arbuscular mycorrhizal (AM) fungi (Glomeromycota) from soil and root DNA. It is optimized for environmental samples with low DNA yield or humic contamination and can be applied prior to long-read sequencing (e.g., PacBio or Nanopore). All steps should be carried out using sterile, DNA-free consumables to minimize contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"r8l3","text":"Prepare all reagents, primers, and consumables in a clean workspace dedicated to pre-PCR operations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lct8","text":"Prepare 70% ethanol and magnetic beads (e.g., AMPure XP) for purification steps in advance.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f25su","text":"Ensure that the thermocycler and magnetic stand are clean and functional before starting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fqnd1","text":"Avoid cross-contamination between PCR rounds; use separate work areas for template preparation and post-PCR handling.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7dkpi","text":"Over-cycling (\u003e35 cycles) may produce chimeric amplicons.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7146n","text":"AMPure beads are sensitive to overdrying—dry only until the surface just turns matte.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cor4i","text":"The long rDNA amplicons (~2.7 kb) are prone to shearing; minimize pipetting and vortexing.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cn1bg","text":"For root DNA, ensure that only fine roots (not woody tissue) are used; otherwise PCR inhibition may occur.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: For soil samples: Extract total genomic DNA from approximately 500 mg of soil using the NucleoSpin Soil DNA Kit (Macherey-Nagel, Germany) according to the manufacturer’s instructions. Perform all steps at room temperature unless otherwise noted, and elute DNA with 50 µL of SE buffer.

For root samples: Extract total gDNA from approximately 20 mg of fine roots using the ISOPLANT II Kit (Nippon Gene Corporation, Tokyo, Japan) following the manufacturer’s protocol. Elute DNA with 50 µL of the supplied buffer.
Measure the extracted gDNA concentration using a Qubit fluorometer with the Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA).

Prepare PCR-master mix in a 0.2 mL PCR tube with the following: Component Volume (total 25 µl) 1 Nuclease-free water X µl 2 Forward primer (NS31, 10 µM) 1.25 µl 3 Reverse primer (LSUmAr13, 10 µM) 0.675 µl 4 Reverse primer (LSUmAr24, 10 µM) 0.675 µl 5 2× KOD One mix 12.5 µl 6 Template DNA Y µl (20 ng)

Prepare PCR-master mix in a 0.2 mL PCR tube with the following: Component Volume (total 25 µl) 1 Nuclease-free water X µl 2 Forward primer (AML1, 10 µl) 1.25 µl 3 Reverse primer (LSUmAR13 10µl) 0.675 µl 4 Reverse primer (LSUmAr24, 10µl) 0.675 µl 5 2 × KOD One mix 12.5 µl 6 Template DNA Y µl (20 ng)
Clean up the 1st PCR products using AMPure XP (Beckman Coulter, USA).

Prepare PCR-master mix in a 0.2 mL PCR tube with the following: Component Volume (total 25 µl) 1 Forward primer (NS31_Glo3) 1.25 µl 2 Reverse primer (wLSUmBr) 1.25 µl 3 2 × KOD One mix 12.5 µl 4 Template DNA (Cleaned 1st PCR products) NS31 / LSUmAr13 + LSUmAr24 5 µl 5 Template DNA (Cleaned 1st PCR products) AML1 / LSUmAr13 + LSUmAr24 5 µl
Equilibrate the beads solution at room temperature and allow the beads to resuspend completely.
Add 12.5 µL beads solution to the 25 µL PCR products (0.5× bead solution/sample ratio) and mix by pipetting up and down multiple (10 to 20) times.
Incubate the tube at room temperature for 5 min.
Place the tube on a magnetic rack (NGS MagnaStand (YS-Model) 8Ch × 0.2 mL PCR tube, Nippon Genetics Co., Ltd., Tokyo, Japan) to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear (~ 00:03:00 min).
Discard the supernatant.
Wash beads by adding 200 µL 70% ethanol.
Incubate the tube on the magnetic rack at room temperature for 00:00:30 .
Discard the supernatant.
Repeat the previous washing steps (8.6-8.8).
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack.
Elute the purified DNA by adding 25 µL of TE Buffer and mix by pipetting.
Incubate the tube at room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Place the tube on the magnetic rack to capture the beads. Incubate until the liquid is clear ( 00:01:00 ).
Transfer the supernatant to a new PCR tube.
Clean up the 2nd PCR products using AMPure XP (0.5× bead solution/sample ratio) following the same procedure as in Section 8.
Measure the DNA concentration of the purified 2nd PCR products using a Qubit fluorometer with the Qubit dsDNA HS assay kit.
OPTIONAL: Check the amplified fragments by gel electrophoresis.
creator: Nobuhiko Shigyo
